content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • • SUITE 2140A • • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

31
A-77-[illegible]

July 26, 1977


Mrs. Edna Warner
71-412 Biskra Road
Rancho Mirage, California 92270

Dear Mrs. Warner:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on 7/25/77,
regarding your off-year campaign disclosure statement, covering the
period from 1/1/77 through 6/30/77.

You stated the following facts: You're a Councilperson in the City
of Rancho Mirage who in February 1977 was the subject of a recall campaign.
Insufficient signatures were gathered on the recall petitions, however,
to qualify the measure for the ballot. In the course of the recall
campaign, you incurred legal fees in the amount of $460. Since the
California Elections Code provides that an officeholder who is the subject
of an unsuccessful recall effort may be reimbursed by the officeholder's
agency for his or her related expenses, you submitted a claim to the City
of Rancho Mirage and received a check for $460. You endorsed the check
and sent it to your attorney. You asked whether the $460 was reportable
on your off-year campaign statement. I advised that you must report the
$460 on form 430, Candidate's Campaign Statement as a "miscellaneous
receipt" on Page 2, Line 3, and as an "expenditure" on line 8. In addition,
you are to attach to the statement an explanation of the source of the
miscellaneous receipt and also on Page 9 of the form state the full
name and address of the payee, in this case your attorney, a description
of the payment and the amount. Enclosed are forms 430 and a campaign
disclosure manual explaining how to complete the remainder of the form.

I hope this information is helpful. If our staff may be of further
assistance, please contact our office.

Sincerely yours,

Nan Emmerson by J.E.
Nan Emmerson
Special Compliance Representative

cc: Rancho Mirage City Clerk

NE:jg

Enc.

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

sections.question: Whether the $460 reimbursement received by an officeholder for expenses incurred during an unsuccessful recall campaign is reportable on an off-year campaign disclosure statement.
sections.conclusion: The $460 must be reported on form 430, Candidate's Campaign Statement as a 'miscellaneous receipt' on Page 2, Line 3, and as an 'expenditure' on Line 8. An explanation of the source of the miscellaneous receipt must be attached, and page 9 of the form must state the full name and address of the payee (the attorney), a description of the service, and the amount.
sections.question_synthetic: A city councilperson received a $460 reimbursement from the city for legal fees incurred during an unsuccessful recall campaign. The question is whether this reimbursement must be reported on the off-year campaign disclosure statement.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The $460 reimbursement is reportable as both a miscellaneous receipt and an expenditure on the candidate's campaign statement (Form 430), with required supporting documentation identifying the attorney and describing the legal services.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation of prior telephone advice dated 7/25/77. OCR quality is moderate with some text degradation; factual content and legal conclusion are clearly extractable. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but the content structure is clear from context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the $460 reimbursement received by an officeholder for expenses incurred during an unsuccessful recall campaign is reportable on an off-year campaign disclosure statement.

CONCLUSION: The $460 must be reported on form 430, Candidate's Campaign Statement as a 'miscellaneous receipt' on Page 2, Line 3, and as an 'expenditure' on Line 8. An explanation of the source of the miscellaneous receipt must be attached, and page 9 of the form must state the full name and address of the payee (the attorney), a description of the service, and the amount.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises a Rancho Mirage city councilperson that a $460 reimbursement for legal fees incurred during an unsuccessful recall campaign must be reported on the off-year campaign disclosure statement (Form 430) with full documentation of the transaction.
